QF.id: I Codes of Conduct and Investor Protection_37

question.text: Under the Investor Protection Fund guidelines, what percentage of the interest or income from IPF received every year must be ploughed back to the IPF?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states two related points: "At least 70% of interest or income received out of any investments made from the IPF" is contributed back; and "At least 70% of interest or income from IPF received every year shall be ploughed back to IPF; 25% can be utilised for promotion of investor education and investor awareness." **The Trap:** 50% might seem like a conservative reasonable reinvestment rate, but SEBI specifies 70% minimum. The remaining 30% is split: 25% for investor education, and 5% for other uses. **Cross-Exam Context:** IPF income allocation: 70% back to IPF corpus + 25% for investor education = 95%. The remaining 5% is unspecified (residual). Exchanges must also conduct half-yearly reviews of IPF adequacy. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IPF reinvestment = 70% minimum; Investor education = 25%. These two numbers together account for 95% of IPF income — memorise both.
